transcript: [doctor] patient shirley taylor date of birth 5/4/1961 mrn 0000123 patient is a 60-year-old right-handed female new patient referred by dr. jacob ward for evaluation of bilateral hand numbness and tingling please note that i personally reviewed the ct brain with and without contrast dated 2/5/2021 no metastatic disease no acute intracranial abnormalities hello mrs. taylor how are you doing this morning hi dr . lopez this weekend was a bit rough i have had this numbness and tingling for a few weeks now so in both of your hands he has ever since last december when i broke them well once the heel i mean i noticed it more with the tingling and numbness you still wearing the splint only on your right wrist no left one also sometimes the right one is just seems to help both of gotten worse over the last month when i broke them though my wrist was definitely in worse shape than it is today i been having some mild stiffness in my neck to hit my head during the accident and they told me it was a contusion okay and where exactly on the right side would you say the tingling and numbness is on the right i can really notice it from the middle and ring finger all the way up to my forearm and on my left a bit more of the pinky and the ring and not the middle rn and is there any pain or or just tingling or numbness mainly just sore sometimes a throb but overall they feel weaker to especially when i'm using them the tingling happens some more but when they hurt you know it goes away and then the tingling is just dull what kind of activities well really i guess just more so at night and then it just kind of comes and goes throughout the day okay i do experience any loss of sensation in your hands or fingers like like feeling no can think of really might be less sensitive than it should be but i can still give me some nerve pain medicine but i have been taking it like i should be okay medical history is notable for chronic kidney disease gout hypertension anxiety gerd and diabetes had prediabetes but i got it under control changing my diet okay good and are you still on the carvedilol 25 mg two times a day for hypertension and lexapro 10 mg a day for anxiety it looks like your allergic to sulfa drugs as well as that correct yeah i'm still taking those and sulfa just causes me to break out in a rash and no history of thyroid issues right no all right thank you mrs. taylor being right back for your exam i will look up that medication you mentioned for your nerve pain ri thanks review of systems please make the following changes constitutional patient reports fatigue and weight gain gi excessive appetite and bloating eyes floaters cardiovascular shortness of breath and leg swelling endocrine positive diabetes always hungry skin nailbed changes dryness itching musculoskeletal gout muscle pain neurological paresthesia bilateral hands please use the default normal neuro physical exam with the following changes sensations normal except pinprick sensation diminished in the third and fourth fingers on the right and the fourth and fifth fingers on the left reflexes are 1+ and symmetric 2+ knees toes are downgoing bilaterally tinel's sign positive at both wrists and both elbows move onto assessment and plan bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome question of bilateral cubital tunnel syndrome i discussed my impression with the patient i will schedule her for emg ncv if her symptoms do not resolve we can consider hand clinic referral for possible symptomatic injections or carpal tunnel release surgery although she can pursue these through her current orthopedist i encouraged her to wear her splints return for emg ncv end of note .
gold_note:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Upper extremity tingling

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

The patient is a 60-year-old female referred today for evaluation of numbness and tingling.

The patient complains of numbness, tingling, and pain in both hands which began last December after she broke both wrists in an MVA and which has progressively worsened since then. She continues to wear a splint on the right wrist all the time and on the left only intermittently, which she states is because the right wrist was injured more severely in the MVA. The sensation radiates from the hands into the forearms and is equal bilaterally. The 4th and 5th fingers in the left hand and 3rd and 4th on the right-hand tingle. She states that her fingers are sore with palpation and seem weaker than before. Any exertion exacerbates the tingling, and after something exacerbates the pain, the tingling seems "dull". The symptoms come and go and seem to occur mostly at night. She denies complete loss of sensation in any of her fingers, but states that they seem less sensitive than they should be. she reports mild stiffness in her neck. She notes that she did hit her head during the MVA and was diagnosed with a contusion. She states that she has been given "nerve pain medicine" but has not been taking it.

The patient takes carvedilol for her hypertension and Lexapro for her anxiety. She reports a history of prediabetes, which she has brought under control via dietary changes. She denies any history of thyroid issues.

REVIEW OF SYMPTOMS

 Constitutional Symptoms: The patient reports increased fatigue and weight gain.
 Eyes: Positive for floaters.
 Cardiovascular: Positive for leg swelling.
 Respiratory: Positive for shortness of breath.
 Gastrointestinal: Positive for excessive hunger and bloating.
 Musculoskeletal: Positive for gout and muscle pain.
 Integumentary: Positive for nail bed changes, dryness, and itching.
 Neurological: Positive for bilateral paresthesia of hands.
 Endocrine: Positive prediabetes and excessive hunger.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY

Chronic kidney disease (CKD).
Prediabetes, diet controlled.
Hypertension.
Gastric esophageal reflux disorder (GERD)

CURRENT MEDICATIONS

Carvedilol 25 mg twice daily.
Lexapro 10 mg daily.

ALLERGIES

Sulfa, causes rash.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Neurological Exam
Sensation is normal except pinprick sensation diminished in the 3rd and 4th fingers on the right and the 4th and 5th fingers on the left. 1+ and symmetric, 2+ knees. Toes are downgoing bilaterally. Tinel's sign positive at both wrists and both elbows.

RESULTS

CT brain with and without contrast, 02/05/2021
Impression: No metastatic disease. No acute intracranial abnormalities. I personally reviewed the report and images.

ASSESSMENT

 Bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome, question of bilateral cubital tunnel syndrome.

PLAN

I discussed my impression with the patient. I will schedule her for EMG/NCV. If her symptoms do not resolve, we can consider Hand Clinic referral for possibly symptomatic injections or carpal tunnel release surgery, although she can also pursue these through her current orthopedist. I encouraged her to wear her splints.

INSTRUCTIONS

Return for EMG/NCV.
notes:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 60-year-old right-handed female, presents with bilateral hand numbness and tingling since December 2020, which worsened over the last month. She reports the numbness and tingling is most pronounced in the middle and ring fingers of her right hand and the pinky and ring fingers of her left hand. She also experiences mild stiffness in her neck, a contusion from a previous accident. The patient reports soreness, a throb, and weakness in her hands, particularly at night. She denies any loss of sensation in her hands or fingers.

Past medical history: The patient has a history of chronic kidney disease, gout, hypertension, anxiety, GERD, and diabetes, which was previously prediabetes but is now under control with dietary changes. She is allergic to sulfa drugs. She is currently taking carvedilol 25 mg twice a day for hypertension and lexapro 10 mg a day for anxiety.

Review of systems: The patient reports fatigue, weight gain, excessive appetite, bloating, floaters, shortness of breath, and leg swelling. She also reports positive symptoms of diabetes, such as always feeling hungry, skin nailbed changes, dryness, and itching. Additionally, she reports musculoskeletal symptoms of gout and muscle pain, and neurological symptoms of paresthesia in her bilateral hands.

Current medications: I prescribed carvedilol 25 mg twice a day for hypertension, and lexapro 10 mg a day for anxiety.

Subjective:

The patient, Shirley Taylor, is a 60-year-old right-handed female who reports bilateral hand numbness and tingling since December, which has worsened over the past month. She denies fever. She reports mild stiffness in her neck and a contusion to the head after a fall, which was evaluated by another physician. She describes the numbness and tingling as starting from the middle and ring finger on the right hand and extending up to her forearm, and on the left hand, it is more pronounced on the pinky and ring finger. She reports soreness, aching, and weakness in her hands, particularly when using them, with tingling that worsens at night. She denies loss of sensation in her hands or fingers, but reports decreased sensitivity. She has been taking carvedilol 25 mg twice daily for hypertension and lexapro 10 mg daily for anxiety. She has a history of sulfa allergy and no thyroid issues. She reports fatigue, weight gain, excessive appetite, bloating, shortness of breath, and leg swelling.

Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]

Subjective:
HPI: Mrs. Taylor is a 60-year-old right-handed female new patient referred for evaluation of bilateral hand numbness and tingling. She reports that the symptoms started in both hands in December, following a wrist injury, and have worsened over the last month. The numbness and tingling are located on the right hand from the middle and ring finger up to the forearm, and on the left hand on the pinky and ring fingers. She denies any pain or sharp sensations, but reports a dull soreness and weakness, especially when using her hands. The symptoms are worse at night and come and go throughout the day. She reports a decrease in sensation in her hands, but no loss of sensation. She has been taking nerve pain medicine, but has not been taking it as prescribed. Past medical history is notable for chronic kidney disease, gout, hypertension, anxiety, GERD, and diabetes, which is now under control. She is allergic to sulfa drugs.

Review of systems: The patient reports fatigue, weight gain, excessive appetite, bloating, shortness of breath, and leg swelling. She also reports positive diabetes, always feeling hungry, skin and nailbed changes, dryness, itching, and gout, muscle pain, and neurological symptoms of paresthesia in both hands.

Current medications: I prescribed carvedilol 25 mg twice a day for hypertension, Lexapro 10 mg a day for anxiety, and nerve pain medicine (unspecified dose and frequency) for nerve pain.

Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]

Physical exam: The physical exam is normal, except for diminished pinprick sensation in the third and fourth fingers on the right and the fourth and fifth fingers on the left. Reflexes are 1+ and symmetric, with downgoing bilaterally. Tinel's sign is positive at both wrists and both elbows.

Test Results: Review of the CT brain with and without contrast dated 2/5/2021 shows no metastatic disease or acute intracranial abnormalities.

Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome, with a question of bilateral cubital tunnel syndrome.

Problem list: Bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome; bilateral cubital tunnel syndrome; chronic kidney disease; gout; hypertension; anxiety; GERD; diabetes.

Plan:
I will schedule the patient for EMG/NCV to assess the extent of her nerve damage. If her symptoms do not resolve, we can consider hand clinic referral for possible symptomatic injections or carpal tunnel release surgery. She can also pursue these options through her current orthopedist. I encouraged her to wear her splints.

Follow-up: I will schedule her for EMG/NCV.

She reports mild stiffness in her neck and a contusion from the fall. She has also experienced some soreness and throbbing in her hands, particularly at night. Past medical history is notable for chronic kidney disease, gout, hypertension, anxiety, GERD, and diabetes, which has been under control since changing her diet. She is allergic to sulfa drugs and is currently taking carvedilol 25 mg twice daily for hypertension and lexapro 10 mg daily for anxiety. She has been taking nerve pain medicine, but the details are not specified.

Current medications: I prescribed carvedilol 25 mg twice daily for hypertension, lexapro 10 mg daily for anxiety, and a medication for nerve pain (dose and frequency not specified) for nerve pain.
